parsed_review.summary: Valid CP00-173 Claude Opus 4.8 max read-only review approved the descriptor-only Risk C bridge pack with two non-blocking P3 observations and no production_ready blockers.

findings.title: Inconsistent descriptor-key naming in CP173 no-write attestation
findings.description: MASTER_DATA_CP173_NO_WRITE_ATTESTATION uses builds_failure_evidence_review_handoff_bridge without the _descriptors suffix used by sibling keys. Registry and contract are self-consistent, validators pass, and there is no functional or boundary impact.
findings.required_action: Optional future naming alignment only; non-blocking.
findings.post_review_disposition: deferred_as_cosmetic: key is self-consistent across registry and contract, no validator or runtime boundary impact, and changing it would create unnecessary contract churn inside CP00-173.

findings.title: Bracket lookup on bridge_sections can resolve prototype members for non-own keys
findings.description: createMasterDataFailureEvidenceReviewHandoffBridgeDescriptor previously used bracket lookup before defaulting. Claude assessed it as defensive hardening only and non-blocking because catalog callers use Object.keys and outputs remain benign.
findings.required_action: Gate section lookup with Object.hasOwn before reading bridge_sections.
findings.post_review_disposition: fixed: createMasterDataFailureEvidenceReviewHandoffBridgeDescriptor now checks Object.hasOwn before section lookup; focused tests and RP04 master-data validation pass afterward.
